landmark_roles.use: Navigation region. Multiple allowed — label each with aria-label.
Primary content. Exactly one per page.
Supporting content (sidebar). Label with aria-label if multiple.
Search region. Apply role='search' to the <form> wrapping a search input.
Form region. Only use when form is significant — needs aria-label or aria-labelledby.
Generic landmark for important sections. Requires aria-label to appear in landmark list.

aria_attributes.use: Provides an invisible text label for an element
aria_attributes.when: Element has no visible text label (icon buttons, nav with no heading, search input)

aria_attributes.use: Points to the element that labels this element
aria_attributes.when: Element's label is a visible heading or other element on screen
Additional context helps assistive tech users understand an element (form hint, tooltip content)
aria_attributes.example: We'll never share your email.

aria_attributes.when: Decorative icons, duplicated text, visual-only elements (loading spinners with text label nearby)

values.polite: Wait for user idle — success messages, counts, form validation
values.assertive: Interrupt immediately — critical errors, alerts only

aria_attributes.rule: Prefer polite. Use assertive only for urgent errors. Never put decorative changes in a live region.

aria_attributes.use: Indicates whether a controlled element (dropdown, accordion, disclosure) is open or closed
Points to the element this element controls
aria_attributes.when: Disclosure triggers, tab buttons, accordion headers, combobox triggers

aria_attributes.use: Marks the current item in a set (breadcrumbs, nav, step indicator)
Marks a form field as required
aria_attributes.note: Still show visual * indicator in label — aria-required is for screen readers only

aria_attributes.use: Marks a form field as having an invalid value
Marks an element as disabled but keeps it in focus order
aria_attributes.difference_from_disabled: HTML disabled removes from focus order. aria-disabled keeps it focusable — better for explaining why it's disabled via tooltip.
aria_attributes.when: Form submit button disabled until valid — keep it focusable so user can tab to it and get hint

aria_attributes.note: Set aria-busy='false' when content finishes loading

screen_reader_patterns.why: Without aria-label, screen reader reads 'button' with no action context
Decorative images: alt=''. Informational images: describe what the image conveys, not its appearance

screen_reader_patterns.right: Email is required
screen_reader_patterns.why: Screen readers don't associate floating error text with an input unless explicitly linked
Pure CSS spinners are invisible to screen readers. Wrap with role='status' and a label.
Screen readers don't announce DOM changes unless a live region is present
Screen reader landmark lists show all nav elements. Labels distinguish them.

color_and_visual: NEVER rely on color alone to convey meaning — always add icon, text label, or pattern
Ensure 4.5:1 contrast for normal text, 3:1 for large text and UI components (WCAG AA)
All interactive elements need a visible focus indicator with at least 3:1 contrast against adjacent colors
Provide text alternatives for all charts, graphs, and data visualizations
Animations must respect prefers-reduced-motion — provide instant/snap alternative

perceivable: All images have alt text (or alt='' if decorative)
Color is not the only visual means of conveying information
Contrast ratio meets 4.5:1 for text, 3:1 for UI components
Text can be resized to 200% without loss of content
operable: No keyboard traps outside of intentional modals
Focus order is logical and follows content flow
Focus indicator is visible on all interactive elements
Users have enough time to read content (no auto-advancing carousels without control)
understandable: Form inputs have labels, hints, and error descriptions
Error messages identify the field and describe how to fix
robust: HTML is valid — no unclosed tags, duplicate IDs
ARIA is used correctly and only when semantic HTML is insufficient
Interactive widgets work with current screen readers (NVDA + Firefox, JAWS + Chrome)
